context: So far your sentences have been single ideas: "Me gusta el cafe." Real conversation joins ideas together. A handful of tiny words - and, but, because - do almost all of that work, and you already know everything they connect.
explanation: Three connectors carry most conversations. Use y (and) to add: "el cafe y el te". Use pero (but) to contrast: "me gusta el cafe pero no me gusta el te". Use porque (because) to give a reason: "estoy aqui porque me gusta España". They simply sit between two ideas you can already say - no new endings to learn.

examples.note: y just adds one thing to another.

examples.es: me gusta el cafe pero no me gusta el te
examples.en: I like coffee but I don't like tea
examples.note: pero flips to the opposite idea.

examples.es: estoy aqui porque me gusta España
examples.en: I'm here because I like Spain

examples.note: tambien adds yourself or another fact.
muy strengthens the next word (bien -> very well).
